summary: Implemented case-insensitive username handling by normalizing all usernames to lowercase at the schema, route, and storage levels, and added a startup normalization routine to merge existing accounts that differ only by case.

plan.title: Normalize usernames in shared schema
plan.description: Updated `insertUserSchema` in `shared/schema.ts` to transform the `username` field to lowercase using Zod's `.transform()`.

plan.title: Normalize username in sharing route
plan.description: Updated the project sharing route in `server/routes.ts` to lowercase the target username before looking it up in the storage.

plan.title: Implement username normalization and merge logic
plan.description: Added `normalizeUsernames` method to `DatabaseStorage` and `FileStorage` to convert existing usernames to lowercase and merge duplicate accounts (transferring project ownership and shares) to solve project visibility issues on mobile.

plan.title: Run normalization on server startup
plan.description: Updated `server/index.ts` to call `storage.normalizeUsernames()` upon server initialization.

plan.title: Improve mobile login and sharing UI
plan.description: Added `autoCapitalize="none"` and `autoComplete="username"` to username input fields in `AuthPage.tsx` and `ShareProjectDialog.tsx` to prevent mobile devices from automatically capitalizing the first letter.

acceptance_criteria.description: insertUserSchema in shared/schema.ts transforms username to lowercase
acceptance_criteria.how_to_verify: Read shared/schema.ts and check for .transform(val => val.toLowerCase()) on the username field

acceptance_criteria.description: POST /api/projects/:id/share route normalizes username to lowercase
acceptance_criteria.how_to_verify: Read server/routes.ts and verify that username from req.body is lowercased before storage lookup

acceptance_criteria.description: DatabaseStorage has normalizeUsernames method that merges users
acceptance_criteria.how_to_verify: Read server/databaseStorage.ts and verify the logic for updating project ownerId and shares during user merge

acceptance_criteria.description: FileStorage has normalizeUsernames method that merges users
acceptance_criteria.how_to_verify: Read server/fileStorage.ts and verify the logic for updating project ownerId and sharedWith array during user merge

acceptance_criteria.description: Server startup routine includes username normalization
acceptance_criteria.how_to_verify: Read server/index.ts and verify that storage.normalizeUsernames() is called in the startup block
Read client/src/pages/AuthPage.tsx and check Input components for autoCapitalize prop
Read client/src/components/editor/ShareProjectDialog.tsx and check the Input component for autoCapitalize prop

acceptance_criteria.description: All existing tests and type checks pass

risks: Merging users might lead to unexpected project lists if the same person created multiple accounts with different projects.
Database conflicts during normalization if unique constraints are strictly enforced in a way that prevents temporary duplicates (handled by logic check).
